metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify that ticket rebar:9597-1b85-d2ab-4eed (the [metrics] extra creator) is retrievable and its plan/file_impact confirms the extra name, contents (lizard only), and that scc/jscpd are excluded — or resolve the ticket ID typo if that is the cause of the lookup failure.
evidence: Plan states: 'a [metrics] pip extra is created by [rebar:9597-1b85-d2ab-4eed] containing only lizard'
show_ticket('rebar:9597-1b85-d2ab-4eed') returned: 'Ticket not found'
Search for '[metrics]' in repo returned no matches in pyproject.toml or elsewhere
pyproject.toml extras confirmed: 'agents', 'tracing', 'grounding', 'grounding-t2', 'dev' — no 'metrics' extra present
findings.finding: The plan asserts that the '[metrics]' pip extra is created by prerequisite ticket [rebar:9597-1b85-d2ab-4eed], but show_ticket returns 'not found' for that ticket ID, preventing affirmative confirmation via the cited-prerequisite escape. The [metrics] extra also does not currently exist anywhere in pyproject.toml. Under fail-closed rules, the plan's relied-upon assumption — that this extra will contain 'lizard' and exclude scc/jscpd — is unbacked, meaning the PROVE assertion in AC1 ('reuse-surface contains "[metrics]"') rests on an unverifiable premise.
scenarios: If ticket 9597-1b85-d2ab-4eed is not actually a prerequisite (edge unverifiable), this ticket documents an extra that hasn't been created, leaving docs ahead of implementation.
If the extra's contents differ from 'lizard only' (e.g., includes scc or jscpd), the documented install instructions will be wrong.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify that ticket rebar:e7a0-acef-ec67-42bb (the [code_health] config section creator) is retrievable and its plan/file_impact enumerates the specific keys, types, and defaults that docs/config.md should document — or resolve the ticket ID lookup failure.
evidence: Plan states: 'the [code_health] config section is created by [rebar:e7a0-acef-ec67-42bb]'
show_ticket('rebar:e7a0-acef-ec67-42bb') returned: 'Ticket not found'
Search for '[code_health]' in repo returned no matches
docs/config.md does not contain '[code_health]' (confirmed by grep — no matches)
findings.finding: The plan asserts that the '[code_health]' config section is created by prerequisite ticket [rebar:e7a0-acef-ec67-42bb], but show_ticket returns 'not found' for that ticket ID, preventing affirmative confirmation via the cited-prerequisite escape. The string '[code_health]' also has zero matches anywhere in the repo. The plan's AC2 PROVE assertion ('docs/config.md contains "[code_health]"') and the new test assertion both depend on this section's keys/types/defaults being defined somewhere implementers can reference.
scenarios: If ticket e7a0-acef-ec67-42bb is not actually a blocker/prerequisite (edge unverifiable), the implementer must document a config section whose keys, types, and defaults are undefined — resulting in speculative documentation.
If the section name or key set differs from what the implementer assumes, the docs and tests will be misaligned with the actual implementation.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Identify and record the correct ticket ID that creates the `[metrics]` pip extra (the plan cites `rebar:9597-1b85-d2ab-4eed` which is not found), add it as an explicit `depends_on` prerequisite, and confirm `pyproject.toml` will contain a `[metrics]` group containing `lizard` before this ticket is implemented.
evidence: Plan states: 'a `[metrics]` pip extra is created by `[rebar:9597-1b85-d2ab-4eed]`'
show_ticket('rebar:9597-1b85-d2ab-4eed') → 'Ticket not found'
pyproject.toml search for 'metrics' in extras → no matches
Ticket 0734 is about 'Migrate code_health readers onto the analyzer' — it does NOT create the `[metrics]` extra
Evidence class: Contradicted (repo pyproject.toml falsifies current existence of `[metrics]` extra)
findings.finding: The plan asserts that the `[metrics]` pip extra (containing `lizard`) is established by prerequisite ticket `rebar:9597-1b85-d2ab-4eed`, but that ticket ID does not resolve in the tracker, and `pyproject.toml` currently has no `metrics` optional-dependency group. The AC tests assert presence of `"[metrics]"` and `"rebar[metrics]"` in docs, and the dependency listed (0734) is about migrating code_health readers — not creating the extra.
findings.location: Acceptance criteria AC1, AC3; 'Dependencies: 0734' section; plan Grounded Facts block
scenarios: If the `[metrics]` extra does not land before this docs ticket is implemented, `pip install rebar[metrics]` will fail for anyone who follows the documented install instructions, making the documentation actively misleading.

The new test assertions (`reuse-surface contains '[metrics]'`, `user-guide contains 'rebar[metrics]'`) will pass as doc string checks, but the documented capability will not be functional, giving a false-green signal.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Anchor AC3's fallback assertion to a specific literal string (e.g. `"Unavailable"` or `"no analyzer → Unavailable"`) so the test assertion and doc prose are deterministically paired.
findings.finding: AC3's proving command is underspecified for the fallback assertion: the plan says 'assert … a no-analyzer/Unavailable phrase' but does not name the exact string the test must grep/check for. A test cannot assert 'a phrase' — it must assert a specific, anchored literal. As written, the executor must invent the phrase AND write matching doc prose, and a reviewer cannot deterministically verify the AC is satisfied.
findings.location: Acceptance criteria — AC3 ('docs/user-guide.md documents … the "no analyzer -> Unavailable" fallback. PROVE: user-guide contains
scenarios: An executor writes 'If no analyzer is installed, the result is marked as N/A' — passes their own test but uses a different string than a reviewer expects, making the AC unverifiable without re-reading the doc
A future regression that changes 'Unavailable' to 'Unavail.' would not be caught if the test asserts a loose phrase
findings.suggested_fix: Change the PROVE clause to: 'user-guide contains "rebar[metrics]" AND "Unavailable"' and add a note that the doc prose must use the exact token `Unavailable` when describing the fallback state, matching the value returned by the analyzer seam.

findings.finding: AC2's proving command (`grep -q "\[code_health\]" docs/config.md`) only verifies the section header exists, not that every [code_health] key has a type and default documented — the gap between the stated criterion and the measurable proof is wide enough that the command can pass while the requirement is unmet.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a grounded-facts line listing the [code_health] keys (discoverable from the config schema/source); update AC2's prove to e.g. `grep -q 'enabled' docs/config.md && grep -q '\[code_health\]' docs/config.md` using a key name that must appear.

evidence: 1 criterion line(s) bundle multiple deliverables with 'and' (split so each is independently verifiable).

citation [rebar:9597-1b85-d2ab-4eed] ('Document the code-health surface for client adoption. GROUNDED FACTS (verified a') names no VERIFIED upstream prerequisite: P neither declares `depends_on -> 9597-1b85-d2ab-4eed` nor is blocked by it. Add a `depends_on` edge to 9597-1b85-d2ab-4eed, ensure 9597-1b85-d2ab-4eed `blocks` this ticket, or remove the citation. Until then the cited symbol is grounded as normal (fails closed).

citation [rebar:e7a0-acef-ec67-42bb] ('` containing only `lizard`; `scc` and `jscpd` are EXTERNAL PATH CLIs (not pip de') names no VERIFIED upstream prerequisite: P neither declares `depends_on -> e7a0-acef-ec67-42bb` nor is blocked by it. Add a `depends_on` edge to e7a0-acef-ec67-42bb, ensure e7a0-acef-ec67-42bb `blocks` this ticket, or remove the citation. Until then the cited symbol is grounded as normal (fails closed).
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.finding: The 'Unavailable-fallback phrase' that the new `test_metrics_surface_documented` test must assert is specified only as `e.g. "no analyzer"` — an illustrative example rather than a fixed string. This leaves the assertion vocabulary undefined: the implementer may choose any phrase, which may conflict with or duplicate the pre-existing `assert "unavailable" in text` in `test_user_guide_has_metrics_section`, or produce a green test that does not actually enforce the intended fallback contract.
findings.location: Plan corrections (round 4) — AC4 ENUMERATED ASSERTIONS, assertion (3): 'an Unavailable-fallback phrase (e.g. "no analyzer")'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace `e.g. "no analyzer"` with a fixed string drawn from the actual CLI output — e.g. `"Unavailable"` (capital-U, matching the metric output enum) — and note that the pre-existing `assert "unavailable" in text` in `test_user_guide_has_metrics_section` already covers lowercase, so the new assertion should target the install/setup prose (e.g. `"rebar[metrics]"` and `"PATH"`) rather than duplicating the fallback-phrase check.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC (or a step in the test) requiring that the old/stale install instructions or partial metrics references are removed/superseded when the new content is written, not merely appended alongside them.
evidence: All ACs are 'contains X' assertions only — no AC requires removing or superseding existing content
Plan says 'document' without specifying whether existing partial/stale content must be replaced
ABSENCE: no AC or step mentions auditing existing doc content for conflicts
findings.finding: The ACs are purely additive string-presence assertions and do not require auditing or removing stale/contradictory content that may already exist in docs/reuse-surface.md, docs/config.md, or docs/user-guide.md (e.g., a prior placeholder install snippet that omits scc/jscpd). If stale content survives alongside new content, the docs will be internally contradictory yet the test will still pass.
findings.location: Acceptance criteria — all three doc ACs; Files likely touched section
scenarios: docs/user-guide.md previously contained a two-part install (pip only) and the new three-part install is added beneath it — both exist, contradicting each other
A prior draft of docs/reuse-surface.md omitted scc/jscpd; the new sentence is appended but old wording implies all tools come from pip
findings.suggested_fix: In the test, add a negative assertion where relevant (e.g., docs/user-guide.md must NOT contain a two-step install block that omits PATH prereqs) OR add an explicit plan step: 'search docs for prior metrics install instructions and replace them.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC / doc requirement specifying per-tool partial-install behavior (e.g., 'if scc is absent from PATH, complexity metrics show Unavailable while lizard metrics still run') so user-guide.md covers partial-adoption non-happy-paths, not only the all-or-nothing cases.
evidence: AC bullet 3: 'user-guide contains "rebar[metrics]" AND a no-analyzer/Unavailable phrase' — only total absence is covered
'the "no analyzer -> Unavailable" fallback' — singular fallback, not per-tool granularity
ABSENCE: no AC or file-touched entry requires documenting partial-install (e.g., lizard present but scc missing) behavior
findings.finding: The docs spec covers the 'all three tools installed' happy path and the 'no analyzer at all → Unavailable' fallback, but does NOT require documenting the partial-install non-happy-path: what happens when `lizard` is pip-installed but `scc` or `jscpd` are missing from PATH (or vice versa). Users adopting incrementally will encounter this state, and the docs as specified leave them without guidance on per-analyzer fallback behavior.
scenarios: User runs `pip install rebar[metrics]` but has not yet installed `scc` — they get partial metric output or silent Unavailable with no doc explaining why
User installs `jscpd` but not `scc` — duplication metrics work but complexity metrics don't; docs give no indication this mixed state is possible or how it surfaces
findings.suggested_fix: Add a bullet to the user-guide.md AC: 'Documents per-analyzer fallback: if any single PATH CLI is absent, only that analyzer's metrics show Unavailable; other analyzers continue normally.' Add a corresponding assertion in test_metrics_docs.py checking for a phrase like 'PATH' and 'Unavailable' in close proximity, or a 'partial install' / 'individual' qualifier.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm whether AGENTS.md or CONTRIBUTING.md (or equivalent dev-setup doc) needs a note that `scc` and `jscpd` must be installed on PATH for local/CI metrics, and add that file to 'Files likely touched' if so.
evidence: 'scc and jscpd are EXTERNAL PATH CLIs (not pip deps, not in the extra)' — grounded fact from plan
Files likely touched lists only end-user docs and the test file
ABSENCE: no mention of AGENTS.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, or any contributor-setup file
findings.finding: The plan targets only end-user-facing docs (reuse-surface.md, config.md, user-guide.md) but does not mention updating contributor/developer-setup docs (e.g., AGENTS.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, or a dev-setup guide) to note that scc and jscpd must be on PATH for local metrics to work. Contributors who run the tool locally will hit silent Unavailable results without this guidance.
findings.location: Files likely touched section; AC 1 (reuse-surface.md) and AC 3 (user-guide.md)
scenarios: A new contributor runs pytest or the CLI locally, gets Unavailable for all code-health metrics, and has no doc to consult
CI setup scripts miss the scc/jscpd install step because it was never captured in contributor docs

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a positive rationale in the Approach section explaining why content is distributed across reuse-surface.md / config.md / user-guide.md (e.g., each doc is already the natural home for its content — reuse-surface owns the API seam, config.md owns the key inventory, user-guide.md owns the install/UX story) rather than consolidated in a new dedicated page.
evidence: Plan states: 'Document … in the existing docs, and EXTEND the existing tests/scripts/test_metrics_docs.py … rather than adding a parallel test.'
Only rationale given is for the test consolidation; no rationale given for three-doc placement vs. a single dedicated page.
G6 criterion: 'The plan states a POSITIVE rationale for the chosen approach (its absence is a finding).'
findings.finding: The Approach section gives a rationale only for the test-consolidation choice ('EXTEND … rather than adding a parallel test') but provides no positive rationale for WHY the documentation content is spread across three existing files rather than consolidated in a single new docs/code-health.md page. Per G6 criterion 4, the absence of a positive rationale for the chosen structural approach is a finding.
scenarios: An implementer uncertain about placement might split content differently (e.g., creating docs/code-health.md) because no rationale anchors the three-doc choice.
A reviewer cannot assess whether the three-doc approach is intentional design or oversight.
findings.suggested_fix: Append to the Approach sentence: '… in the existing docs (reuse-surface.md is already the API seam reference that includes §5 metrics; config.md is the key-inventory home; user-guide.md is the install/UX doc), keeping each doc as the single authoritative home for its concern rather than creating a new cross-cutting page.'

findings.finding: AGENTS.md is listed in the Files section but has no acceptance criterion, no assertion, and no proving command. An executor cannot know what to write there or how to verify the change is correct.
findings.location: Files: docs/reuse-surface.md, docs/config.md, docs/user-guide.md, AGENTS.md, tests/scripts/test_metrics_docs.py (extend)
findings.suggested_fix: Add: '- [ ] AGENTS.md documents the `[metrics]` extra and PATH prereqs for contributors. PROVE: `grep -q "\[metrics\]" AGENTS.md`' OR remove AGENTS.md from the Files list.

findings.finding: AGENTS.md is listed in the Files section as a deliverable but has no corresponding acceptance criterion to verify its content — it is an orphan deliverable with no observable done-definition.
findings.location: Files section: 'docs/reuse-surface.md, docs/config.md, docs/user-guide.md, AGENTS.md, tests/scripts/test_metrics_docs.py (extend)'

findings.finding: AGENTS.md is listed in the Files section but has no corresponding acceptance criterion, no specified content guidance, and no proof command — an implementer has no enforceable target for what to write there.
findings.location: Files list: `AGENTS.md`; Acceptance criteria section (no AC covering AGENTS.md)
findings.suggested_fix: Either: (a) add an AC such as 'AGENTS.md documents the three-part metrics install for agent context. PROVE: `grep -q "metrics" AGENTS.md`' and include a corresponding assertion in `test_metrics_docs.py`; or (b) if AGENTS.md requires only a brief cross-reference, document that explicitly and drop the file from the Files list if no substantive change is planned.

findings.finding: The Unavailable-fallback phrase for user-guide.md is described as 'e.g. "no analyzer"', making it ambiguous whether 'no analyzer' is the required exact string — yet the Plan Corrections test assertion checks for that specific string. An executor who uses a different phrasing (e.g. 'Unavailable') will produce readable docs but a failing test.
findings.location: Plan Corrections — AC4 assertion: 'an Unavailable-fallback phrase (e.g. "no analyzer")'
findings.suggested_fix: Change to: 'must contain the phrase "no analyzer" (exact string checked by the test)'

findings.finding: AC1's PROVE grep command omits `jscpd`, making it weaker than the round-4 enumerated assertion (1), which requires `docs/reuse-surface.md` to contain `[metrics]` AND `scc` AND `jscpd`. An implementer following only the AC-level PROVE command could ship a reuse-surface.md that mentions `[metrics]` and `scc` but silently omits `jscpd`, pass the PROVE grep, yet fail the actual test assertion in `test_metrics_surface_documented`.
findings.location: Acceptance criteria, AC1 — PROVE command; Plan corrections round-4, assertion (1)
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the AC1 PROVE line with: `grep -q "\[metrics\]" docs/reuse-surface.md && grep -q "scc" docs/reuse-surface.md && grep -q "jscpd" docs/reuse-surface.md`

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
